No Poverty.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus supports contributions to eradicating poverty.

Zero Hunger.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus supports contributions to ending hunger or improving food security.

Good Health and Well-being.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus supports contributions to health or well-being outcomes.

Quality Education.reason: As a university course, it provides tertiary education (4.3) and develops technical skills in programming and emerging technologies for employment (4.4).
evidence: provide the basic knowledge of computer science

Gender Equality.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus supports contributions to gender equality.

Clean Water and Sanitation.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus supports contributions to water or sanitation.

Affordable and Clean Energy.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus supports contributions to affordable and clean energy.

Decent Work and Economic Growth.reason: The course develops skills in programming and emerging technologies, fostering technological upgrading and innovation for economic productivity (Target 8.2).
evidence: programming languages (e.g., HTML, CSS and JavaScript)

Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure.reason: The course covers computer science fundamentals, emerging technologies, and Internet & Network, directly supporting innovation, technological capabilities (9.5), and ICT access (9.c).

Reduced Inequalities.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus supports contributions to reducing inequalities in the SDG sense.

Sustainable Cities and Communities.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus supports contributions to sustainable cities or communities.

Responsible Consumption and Production.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus supports contributions to responsible consumption or production patterns.

Climate Action.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus supports contributions to climate action.

Life Below Water.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus supports contributions to marine life or ocean health.

Life on Land.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus supports contributions to terrestrial ecosystems or biodiversity.

Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions.reason: The course covers 'Digital Security and Privacy' and 'Social responsibility and ethics', relating to protecting freedoms and responsible digital conduct (Target 16.10).

Partnerships for the Goals.reason: The course enhances capacity in information and communications technology (ICT) through programming languages, an enabling technology for development (Target 17.8).
